response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion:
As a 52-year-old man who works as a retail worker in Kansas City, I believe that the best way to manage and deal with fake content on social media is to be proactive. Here are some steps you can take: 
Firstly, educate yourself on how to identify fake news and propaganda. This will help you to recognize when you are seeing false information online. Secondly, fact-check everything you come across on social media before sharing it with others. Thirdly, use reputable sources for news and information, such as The New York Times or BBC News. Fourthly, be cautious of sensationalized headlines and clickbait articles. Finally, consider muting or blocking accounts that constantly post false information. By taking these steps, you can help to protect yourself and others from the harmful effects of fake content on social media.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: Based on my personal experience and research, I believe that being proactive is key to managing and dealing with fake content on social media. By educating oneself, fact-checking information, using reputable sources, being cautious of sensationalized headlines, and muting or blocking accounts that post false information, individuals can help protect themselves and others from the harmful effects of fake content online.
